desc: Mostar is one of the most visually arresting towns in Europe — a living relic of Ottoman Bosnia where the iconic Stari Most (Old Bridge) arches over the turquoise Neretva river between minarets and medieval stone towers. The old bazaar Kujundžiluk is a warren of copper workshops, rug merchants and traditional teahouses. The surrounding landscape is dramatic: limestone karst, emerald river canyons and the vine-terraced Herzegovinian countryside. Despite its UNESCO profile, Mostar remains genuinely unspoilt outside the brief July–August peak.

todo.name: Stari Most (Old Bridge)
todo.desc: The 16th-century Ottoman bridge is one of Europe's most beautiful sights — best at dusk when the stone turns amber.

todo.name: Kujundžiluk bazaar
todo.desc: Ancient cobblestoned market street lined with copper smiths, hand-woven rugs and coffee houses. Very authentic.

todo.name: Koski Mehmed Pasha Mosque
todo.desc: Climb the minaret for the finest panorama over the Old Bridge and Neretva valley.

todo.name: Blagaj Tekke
todo.desc: A 16th-century Dervish monastery at the foot of a 200 m cliff where the Buna river springs from rock. Unmissable.

todo.name: Počitelj village
todo.desc: Perfectly preserved medieval Ottoman fortified village perched above the Neretva. 25 min south.

todo.name: Kravice waterfalls
todo.desc: A 25 m natural amphitheatre of cascades into a turquoise pool — Herzegovina's most beautiful natural site.

todo.name: Neretva river swimming
todo.desc: The crystal-clear Neretva below the Old Bridge is swimmable in summer and startlingly beautiful.

todo.name: Herzegovinian wine tasting
todo.desc: Žilavka white and Blatina red are indigenous grapes found nowhere else. Winery visits around Čitluk are excellent.

wifi.note: Adequate for video calls. Hotel and cafe WiFi reliable in the old town. No major coworking spaces, but local cafes work well.

prac.fltNote: No direct Gatwick flight to Mostar (OMO). Best options: LGW → Split (SPU) with easyJet then ~2.5 hr drive; or LGW → Dubrovnik (DBV) with easyJet then ~2.5 hr drive. Both routes offer direct Gatwick connections.
prac.visa: Visa-free for UK citizens — up to 90 days

sweet: May and September are the sweet spot — Mediterranean warmth without July/August peak crowds or prices.
